forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTBN925WPAWDYXS12W5HETEH
+- 01KTBNHSR4EVTVJ35MGGD510R2
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8980'
+project: local-review
+title: 'Engine stage 1 — scope: build the per-dimension work-list from a git diff + matched rules'
+---
+## What
+First engine stage in `swissarmyhammer-validators::review`. Given a review scope, produce a `WorkList`: per dimension, the set of changed files + their semantic diffs + the matched rules + the tool affordances the fleet agent for that dimension will use. This is what replaces "one tired agent grinds all files and all layers."
+
+- Input: a scope — one of `{ range: "HEAD~4..HEAD" }`, `{ branch: "feature-x" }`, or auto-detect (uncommitted/parent), mirroring the git tool's `get changes` semantics.
+- Reuse the git tool internals (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/.../git/changes` + `git/diff`) — do NOT shell out to git or reimplement diffing. If those live in `swissarmyhammer-tools`, call them as a library or factor the shared git-ops into a place the engine can use; prefer reusing `swissarmyhammer-common` git ops already underneath the tool.
+- For each changed file, call `match_rules(file_path)` (engine API) to get the applicable rule set, and group rules by dimension (per the design taxonomy).
+- Emit `WorkList { dimensions: Vec<DimensionWork { dimension, files: Vec<FileChange { path, semantic_diff }>, rules: Vec<MatchedRule> }> }`. A dimension with no matched files/rules is omitted.
+- Do NOT call the LLM here — this stage is pure data assembly so it is fast and unit-testable without an agent.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `scope_review(scope) -> WorkList` exists in the engine, with no agent/LLM dependency.
+- [ ] Given a range, it returns the changed files with their semantic diffs grouped under the correct dimensions via `match_rules`.
+- [ ] Dimensions with zero applicable files are absent from the `WorkList`.
+- [ ] Reuses git changes/diff and the rule matcher; no re-implemented git or glob logic.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test against a real temp git repo (create commits, change a `.rs` file): assert the `WorkList` contains the file under the source-code dimensions with a non-empty semantic diff.
+- [ ] Test that an unmatched file type (e.g. a `.lock`) produces no dimension work.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::scope` green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — build the temp-repo fixture and assert the `WorkList` shape first. Reuse git ops and `match_rules`; if the git tool code is not yet library-callable from the engine, the smallest correct move is to factor the git-ops call site so both the tool and the engine share it (note it in the design doc).
\ No newline at end of file

If those live in `swissarmyhammer-tools`, call them as a library or factor the shared git-ops into a place the engine can use; prefer reusing `swissarmyhammer-common` git ops already underneath the tool.
-- For each changed file, call `match_rules(file_path)` (engine API) to get the applicable rule set, and group rules by dimension (per the design taxonomy).

\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -7,27 +7,34 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '8980'
 project: local-review
-title: 'Engine stage 1 — scope: build the per-dimension work-list from a git diff + matched rules'
+title: 'Engine stage 1 — scope: resolve file/glob/working/sha to a per-validator work-list'
 ---
 ## What
-First engine stage in `swissarmyhammer-validators::review`. Given a review scope, produce a `WorkList`: per dimension, the set of changed files + their semantic diffs + the matched rules + the tool affordances the fleet agent for that dimension will use. This is what replaces "one tired agent grinds all files and all layers."
+First engine stage in `swissarmyhammer-validators::review`. Given a review scope, produce a `WorkList`: per matched validator, the set of files + their content/semantic-diff + the validator's rules and probes. The validator is the shard — this is what replaces "one tired agent grinds all files and all concerns."

-- Input: a scope — one of `{ range: "HEAD~4..HEAD" }`, `{ branch: "feature-x" }`, or auto-detect (uncommitted/parent), mirroring the git tool's `get changes` semantics.
-- Reuse the git tool internals (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/.../git/changes` + `git/diff`) — do NOT shell out to git or reimplement diffing. If those live in `swissarmyhammer-tools`, call them as a library or factor the shared git-ops into a place the engine can use; prefer reusing `swissarmyhammer-common` git ops already underneath the tool.
-- For each changed file, call `match_rules(file_path)` (engine API) to get the applicable rule set, and group rules by dimension (per the design taxonomy).
-- Emit `WorkList { dimensions: Vec<DimensionWork { dimension, files: Vec<FileChange { path, semantic_diff }>, rules: Vec<MatchedRule> }> }`. A dimension with no matched files/rules is omitted.
-- Do NOT call the LLM here — this stage is pure data assembly so it is fast and unit-testable without an agent.
+- Input: a scope — exactly one of:
+  - `working` — uncommitted changes vs HEAD (staged + unstaged + untracked); the default.
+  - `sha` — changes in/since a commit or range (reuse the git tool's `get changes` range semantics).
+  - `file` — a single file path: its changes if any, else whole-file content.
+  - `glob` — all files matching a pattern.
+  `working`/`sha` are diff scopes (changed files + semantic diff + surrounding context); `file`/`glob` are explicit-set scopes (the file set is the glob/file match, reviewed whole when there is no diff).
+- Reuse the git tool internals (`git/changes` + `git/diff`) for the diff scopes — do NOT shell out or reimplement diffing; prefer the `swissarmyhammer-common` git ops already underneath the tool. For `file`/`glob`, resolve the file set directly and read content.
+- For each file, call `match_rules(file_path)` (engine API) to get the validators whose globs match, and assemble per-validator work.
+- Emit `WorkList { validators: Vec<ValidatorWork { validator_name, severity, probes, files: Vec<FileChange { path, content_or_diff }>, rules: Vec<Rule> }> }`. A validator with no matched files is omitted.
+- Do NOT call the LLM here — pure data assembly, fast and unit-testable without an agent.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `scope_review(scope) -> WorkList` exists in the engine, with no agent/LLM dependency.
-- [ ] Given a range, it returns the changed files with their semantic diffs grouped under the correct dimensions via `match_rules`.
-- [ ] Dimensions with zero applicable files are absent from the `WorkList`.
+- [ ] `scope_review(scope) -> WorkList` exists, no agent/LLM dependency; accepts exactly one of `file`/`glob`/`working`/`sha` (zero/multiple → error).
+- [ ] Diff scopes return changed files with semantic diffs; explicit-set scopes return the matched files with content.
+- [ ] Files are grouped under the matching validators via `match_rules`; validators with zero matched files are absent.
 - [ ] Reuses git changes/diff and the rule matcher; no re-implemented git or glob logic.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Integration test against a real temp git repo (create commits, change a `.rs` file): assert the `WorkList` contains the file under the source-code dimensions with a non-empty semantic diff.
-- [ ] Test that an unmatched file type (e.g. a `.lock`) produces no dimension work.
+- [ ] Integration test against a real temp git repo: change a `.rs` file, run with `working` → the file appears under the validators whose globs match `.rs`, with a non-empty semantic diff.
+- [ ] `sha` test: commit the change, scope by the commit range → same file set.
+- [ ] `glob` test: `**/*.rs` returns the matched files as whole-content work even with no diff.
+- [ ] An unmatched file type (e.g. a `.lock`) produces no validator work.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::scope` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — build the temp-repo fixture and assert the `WorkList` shape first. Reuse git ops and `match_rules`; if the git tool code is not yet library-callable from the engine, the smallest correct move is to factor the git-ops call site so both the tool and the engine share it (note it in the design doc).
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — build the temp-repo fixture and assert the `WorkList` shape per scope kind first. Reuse git ops and `match_rules`; if the git tool code is not library-callable from the engine, factor the shared git-ops call site so both the tool and engine use it (note it in the design doc).
\ No newline at end of file
reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -7,34 +7,27 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '8980'
 project: local-review
-title: 'Engine stage 1 — scope: resolve file/glob/working/sha to a per-validator work-list'
+title: 'Engine stage 1 — scope: build the per-dimension work-list from a git diff + matched rules'
 ---
 ## What
-First engine stage in `swissarmyhammer-validators::review`. Given a review scope, produce a `WorkList`: per matched validator, the set of files + their content/semantic-diff + the validator's rules and probes. The validator is the shard — this is what replaces "one tired agent grinds all files and all concerns."
+First engine stage in `swissarmyhammer-validators::review`. Given a review scope, produce a `WorkList`: per dimension, the set of changed files + their semantic diffs + the matched rules + the tool affordances the fleet agent for that dimension will use. This is what replaces "one tired agent grinds all files and all layers."

-- Input: a scope — exactly one of:
-  - `working` — uncommitted changes vs HEAD (staged + unstaged + untracked); the default.
-  - `sha` — changes in/since a commit or range (reuse the git tool's `get changes` range semantics).
-  - `file` — a single file path: its changes if any, else whole-file content.
-  - `glob` — all files matching a pattern.
-  `working`/`sha` are diff scopes (changed files + semantic diff + surrounding context); `file`/`glob` are explicit-set scopes (the file set is the glob/file match, reviewed whole when there is no diff).
-- Reuse the git tool internals (`git/changes` + `git/diff`) for the diff scopes — do NOT shell out or reimplement diffing; prefer the `swissarmyhammer-common` git ops already underneath the tool. For `file`/`glob`, resolve the file set directly and read content.
-- For each file, call `match_rules(file_path)` (engine API) to get the validators whose globs match, and assemble per-validator work.
-- Emit `WorkList { validators: Vec<ValidatorWork { validator_name, severity, probes, files: Vec<FileChange { path, content_or_diff }>, rules: Vec<Rule> }> }`. A validator with no matched files is omitted.
-- Do NOT call the LLM here — pure data assembly, fast and unit-testable without an agent.
+- Input: a scope — one of `{ range: "HEAD~4..HEAD" }`, `{ branch: "feature-x" }`, or auto-detect (uncommitted/parent), mirroring the git tool's `get changes` semantics.
+- Reuse the git tool internals (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/.../git/changes` + `git/diff`) — do NOT shell out to git or reimplement diffing. If those live in `swissarmyhammer-tools`, call them as a library or factor the shared git-ops into a place the engine can use; prefer reusing `swissarmyhammer-common` git ops already underneath the tool.
+- For each changed file, call `match_rules(file_path)` (engine API) to get the applicable rule set, and group rules by dimension (per the design taxonomy).
+- Emit `WorkList { dimensions: Vec<DimensionWork { dimension, files: Vec<FileChange { path, semantic_diff }>, rules: Vec<MatchedRule> }> }`. A dimension with no matched files/rules is omitted.
+- Do NOT call the LLM here — this stage is pure data assembly so it is fast and unit-testable without an agent.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `scope_review(scope) -> WorkList` exists, no agent/LLM dependency; accepts exactly one of `file`/`glob`/`working`/`sha` (zero/multiple → error).
-- [ ] Diff scopes return changed files with semantic diffs; explicit-set scopes return the matched files with content.
-- [ ] Files are grouped under the matching validators via `match_rules`; validators with zero matched files are absent.
+- [ ] `scope_review(scope) -> WorkList` exists in the engine, with no agent/LLM dependency.
+- [ ] Given a range, it returns the changed files with their semantic diffs grouped under the correct dimensions via `match_rules`.
+- [ ] Dimensions with zero applicable files are absent from the `WorkList`.
 - [ ] Reuses git changes/diff and the rule matcher; no re-implemented git or glob logic.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Integration test against a real temp git repo: change a `.rs` file, run with `working` → the file appears under the validators whose globs match `.rs`, with a non-empty semantic diff.
-- [ ] `sha` test: commit the change, scope by the commit range → same file set.
-- [ ] `glob` test: `**/*.rs` returns the matched files as whole-content work even with no diff.
-- [ ] An unmatched file type (e.g. a `.lock`) produces no validator work.
+- [ ] Integration test against a real temp git repo (create commits, change a `.rs` file): assert the `WorkList` contains the file under the source-code dimensions with a non-empty semantic diff.
+- [ ] Test that an unmatched file type (e.g. a `.lock`) produces no dimension work.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::scope` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — build the temp-repo fixture and assert the `WorkList` shape per scope kind first. Reuse git ops and `match_rules`; if the git tool code is not library-callable from the engine, factor the shared git-ops call site so both the tool and engine use it (note it in the design doc).
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — build the temp-repo fixture and assert the `WorkList` shape first. Reuse git ops and `match_rules`; if the git tool code is not yet library-callable from the engine, the smallest correct move is to factor the git-ops call site so both the tool and the engine share it (note it in the design doc).
\ No newline at end of file

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -4,6 +4,7 @@
 depends_on:
 - 01KTBN925WPAWDYXS12W5HETEH
 - 01KTBNHSR4EVTVJ35MGGD510R2
+- 01KTBQR87DKQF750JTJ3G52FZR
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '8980'
 project: local-review
@@ -10,7 +11,7 @@
 title: 'Engine stage 1 — scope: resolve file/glob/working/sha to a per-validator work-list'
 ---
 ## What
-First engine stage in `swissarmyhammer-validators::review`. Given a review scope, produce a `WorkList`: per matched validator, the set of files + their content/semantic-diff + the validator's rules and probes. The validator is the shard — this is what replaces "one tired agent grinds all files and all concerns."
+First engine stage in `swissarmyhammer-validators::review`. Given a review scope, produce a `WorkList`: per matched validator, the files + their content/semantic-diff + the validator's rules + **the pre-run probe results** (engine-executed, ground-truth evidence). The validator is the shard. Running probes here (not in the agent) is what guarantees the duplication/dead-code evidence is always present — fixing the "agent skips the probe under load" failure.

 - Input: a scope — exactly one of:
   - `working` — uncommitted changes vs HEAD (staged + unstaged + untracked); the default.
@@ -17,24 +18,24 @@
   - `sha` — changes in/since a commit or range (reuse the git tool's `get changes` range semantics).
   - `file` — a single file path: its changes if any, else whole-file content.
   - `glob` — all files matching a pattern.
-  `working`/`sha` are diff scopes (changed files + semantic diff + surrounding context); `file`/`glob` are explicit-set scopes (the file set is the glob/file match, reviewed whole when there is no diff).
-- Reuse the git tool internals (`git/changes` + `git/diff`) for the diff scopes — do NOT shell out or reimplement diffing; prefer the `swissarmyhammer-common` git ops already underneath the tool. For `file`/`glob`, resolve the file set directly and read content.
-- For each file, call `match_rules(file_path)` (engine API) to get the validators whose globs match, and assemble per-validator work.
-- Emit `WorkList { validators: Vec<ValidatorWork { validator_name, severity, probes, files: Vec<FileChange { path, content_or_diff }>, rules: Vec<Rule> }> }`. A validator with no matched files is omitted.
-- Do NOT call the LLM here — pure data assembly, fast and unit-testable without an agent.
+  `working`/`sha` are diff scopes (changed files + semantic diff + surrounding context); `file`/`glob` are explicit-set scopes (reviewed whole when there is no diff).
+- Reuse the git tool internals (`git/changes` + `git/diff`) for diff scopes — do NOT shell out or reimplement diffing; prefer the `swissarmyhammer-common` git ops under the tool. For `file`/`glob`, resolve the file set directly and read content.
+- For each file, call `match_rules(file_path)` to get the validators whose globs match, and assemble per-validator work.
+- For each matched validator, run its declared `probes` via the probe registry (`run_probes`) over that validator's files/changed-symbols, and attach the structured `probe_results` to the work item. This stage uses code_context (not an LLM) — still deterministic and unit-testable without an agent.
+- Emit `WorkList { validators: Vec<ValidatorWork { validator_name, severity, files: Vec<FileChange { path, content_or_diff, changed_symbols }>, rules: Vec<Rule>, probe_results: ProbeResults }> }`. A validator with no matched files is omitted.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `scope_review(scope) -> WorkList` exists, no agent/LLM dependency; accepts exactly one of `file`/`glob`/`working`/`sha` (zero/multiple → error).
-- [ ] Diff scopes return changed files with semantic diffs; explicit-set scopes return the matched files with content.
-- [ ] Files are grouped under the matching validators via `match_rules`; validators with zero matched files are absent.
-- [ ] Reuses git changes/diff and the rule matcher; no re-implemented git or glob logic.
+- [ ] Diff scopes return changed files with semantic diffs + changed symbols; explicit-set scopes return matched files with content.
+- [ ] Files are grouped under matching validators via `match_rules`; each validator's `probes` are run and attached as `probe_results`.
+- [ ] Reuses git changes/diff, `match_rules`, and the probe registry; no re-implemented git/glob/probe logic.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Integration test against a real temp git repo: change a `.rs` file, run with `working` → the file appears under the validators whose globs match `.rs`, with a non-empty semantic diff.
-- [ ] `sha` test: commit the change, scope by the commit range → same file set.
-- [ ] `glob` test: `**/*.rs` returns the matched files as whole-content work even with no diff.
+- [ ] Integration test against a real temp git repo + code_context index: change a `.rs` file adding a duplicate function, run with `working` → the file appears under the matching validators with a non-empty semantic diff AND the duplication validator's `probe_results` contain the `find_duplicates` hit.
+- [ ] `sha` test: commit the change, scope by the commit range → same file set + probe results.
+- [ ] `glob` test: `**/*.rs` returns matched files as whole-content work even with no diff.
 - [ ] An unmatched file type (e.g. a `.lock`) produces no validator work.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::scope` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — build the temp-repo fixture and assert the `WorkList` shape per scope kind first. Reuse git ops and `match_rules`; if the git tool code is not library-callable from the engine, factor the shared git-ops call site so both the tool and engine use it (note it in the design doc).
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — assert the `WorkList` shape (incl. probe_results) per scope kind first. Reuse git ops, `match_rules`, and `run_probes`; if the git tool code is not library-callable from the engine, factor the shared git-ops call site so both the tool and engine use it (note it in the design doc).
\ No newline at end of file

 - [ ] An unmatched file type (e.g. a `.lock`) produces no validator work.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::scope` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — assert the `WorkList` shape (incl. probe_results) per scope kind first. Reuse git ops, `match_rules`, and `run_probes`; if the git tool code is not library-callable from the engine, factor the shared git-ops call site so both the tool and engine use it (note it in the design doc).
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@
 title: 'Engine stage 1 — scope: resolve file/glob/working/sha to a per-validator work-list'
 ---
 ## What
-First engine stage in `swissarmyhammer-validators::review`. Given a review scope, produce a `WorkList`: per matched validator, the files + their content/semantic-diff + the validator's rules + **the pre-run probe results** (engine-executed, ground-truth evidence). The validator is the shard. Running probes here (not in the agent) is what guarantees the duplication/dead-code evidence is always present — fixing the "agent skips the probe under load" failure.
+First engine stage in `swissarmyhammer-validators::review`. Given a review scope, produce a `WorkList`: the review-level change purpose, plus per matched validator the files to review — each file carrying its structured diff, a bounded slice of source (NOT the whole file), and engine-run probe evidence. The validator is the shard; the **file is the grain** (the agent reviews one file at a time). This stage uses git + code_context only (no LLM) — deterministic and unit-testable.

 - Input: a scope — exactly one of:
   - `working` — uncommitted changes vs HEAD (staged + unstaged + untracked); the default.
@@ -18,24 +18,40 @@
   - `sha` — changes in/since a commit or range (reuse the git tool's `get changes` range semantics).
   - `file` — a single file path: its changes if any, else whole-file content.
   - `glob` — all files matching a pattern.
-  `working`/`sha` are diff scopes (changed files + semantic diff + surrounding context); `file`/`glob` are explicit-set scopes (reviewed whole when there is no diff).
-- Reuse the git tool internals (`git/changes` + `git/diff`) for diff scopes — do NOT shell out or reimplement diffing; prefer the `swissarmyhammer-common` git ops under the tool. For `file`/`glob`, resolve the file set directly and read content.
-- For each file, call `match_rules(file_path)` to get the validators whose globs match, and assemble per-validator work.
-- For each matched validator, run its declared `probes` via the probe registry (`run_probes`) over that validator's files/changed-symbols, and attach the structured `probe_results` to the work item. This stage uses code_context (not an LLM) — still deterministic and unit-testable without an agent.
-- Emit `WorkList { validators: Vec<ValidatorWork { validator_name, severity, files: Vec<FileChange { path, content_or_diff, changed_symbols }>, rules: Vec<Rule>, probe_results: ProbeResults }> }`. A validator with no matched files is omitted.
+- Reuse the git tool internals (`git/changes` + `git/diff`) for diff scopes; for `file`/`glob` resolve the file set directly. Do NOT shell out or reimplement diffing.
+- **Change purpose**: gather the review-level intent once — commit message(s) for `sha`, the kanban task title+body when invoked task-mode, else a short auto summary. Attach to the `WorkList` (shared by every agent).
+- For each file, call `match_rules(file_path)` to get matching validators; for each matched validator run its declared `probes` via the registry over that file's changed entities and attach the results.
+- **`FileChange` carries a BOUNDED slice, not the whole file** (token discipline — the agent reviews one file but a 2000-line file should not be dumped): the file header (imports / module decl), each changed entity's full source (entity boundaries come from the semantic diff), and a small window (~40 lines) around each hunk. For small files this is effectively the whole file.
+
+Emit:
+```
+WorkList {
+  change_purpose: String,
+  validators: Vec<ValidatorWork {
+    validator_name, severity, rules: Vec<Rule>, probes: Vec<ProbeName>,
+    files: Vec<FileChange {
+      path,
+      semantic_diff,            // changed entities added/modified/deleted, before→after
+      changed_symbols,
+      source_slice,             // header + changed entities + window — NOT whole file
+      probe_results,            // callers / duplicates / similar for this file's entities
+    }>,
+  }>,
+}
+```
+A validator with no matched files is omitted.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `scope_review(scope) -> WorkList` exists, no agent/LLM dependency; accepts exactly one of `file`/`glob`/`working`/`sha` (zero/multiple → error).
-- [ ] Diff scopes return changed files with semantic diffs + changed symbols; explicit-set scopes return matched files with content.
-- [ ] Files are grouped under matching validators via `match_rules`; each validator's `probes` are run and attached as `probe_results`.
-- [ ] Reuses git changes/diff, `match_rules`, and the probe registry; no re-implemented git/glob/probe logic.
+- [ ] `WorkList.change_purpose` is populated from commit/task context.
+- [ ] Each `FileChange` carries the structured semantic diff, a bounded `source_slice` (header + changed entities + window, NOT the whole file), changed symbols, and the validator's `probe_results`.
+- [ ] Files grouped under matching validators via `match_rules`; reuses git changes/diff, `match_rules`, `run_probes` — no re-implemented git/glob/probe logic.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Integration test against a real temp git repo + code_context index: change a `.rs` file adding a duplicate function, run with `working` → the file appears under the matching validators with a non-empty semantic diff AND the duplication validator's `probe_results` contain the `find_duplicates` hit.
-- [ ] `sha` test: commit the change, scope by the commit range → same file set + probe results.
-- [ ] `glob` test: `**/*.rs` returns matched files as whole-content work even with no diff.
-- [ ] An unmatched file type (e.g. a `.lock`) produces no validator work.
+- [ ] Integration test (real temp git repo + code_context index): change a `.rs` file adding a duplicate function; `working` scope → the file appears under matching validators with a non-empty semantic diff, a `source_slice` that includes the changed function + imports but NOT unrelated distant code, and the duplication validator's `probe_results` containing the `find_duplicates` hit.
+- [ ] `change_purpose` is set from the commit message under `sha` scope.
+- [ ] `glob` test: `**/*.rs` returns matched files as whole-content work even with no diff; an unmatched `.lock` yields no validator work.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::scope` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — assert the `WorkList` shape (incl. probe_results) per scope kind first. Reuse git ops, `match_rules`, and `run_probes`; if the git tool code is not library-callable from the engine, factor the shared git-ops call site so both the tool and engine use it (note it in the design doc).
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — assert the `WorkList` shape (change_purpose, bounded source_slice, probe_results) first. Reuse git ops, `match_rules`, `run_probes`, and the semantic differ's entity boundaries for the slice; if the git tool is not library-callable from the engine, factor the shared git-ops call site (note it in the design doc).
\ No newline at end of file
